diary: Monday started clear and warm—one of those June mornings that makes you remember why people move to Seattle. I was up early for my shift, coffee in hand, watching the light come through the kitchen window in Rainier Valley before heading to Capitol Hill. The dispatch center was buzzing this morning; apparently the World Cup match had half the bars downtown prepping for the 11am kickoff, and the Light Rail closure meant more traffic than usual. I handled the usual symphony of calls—nothing major, thankfully. Grabbed a quick lunch at this little deli I like, then headed home to steal a few hours of quiet. Did laundry, folded things slowly, let my mind settle. By late afternoon I was itching to move, so I drove down to the climbing gym. Those couple hours on the wall felt like exactly what I needed—just my hands, the holds, the focus. Everything else fell away. Drove home, showered, caught the tail end of the evening, and made it to Foundation for some dancing before heading back to Rainier Valley. Tired now, but the good kind.
thought_process: Today felt like it needed balance, and I think I found it. The morning dispatch shift was steady—the World Cup buzz and transit closure added texture but nothing I couldn't handle. I made the call to head home mid-afternoon instead of staying in Capitol Hill; I was feeling a bit drained and needed that reset time. The climbing gym was non-negotiable for me today—I've learned that when I'm stuck in my head during calls, I need my body to do something real and immediate. Dancing later felt natural, a way to close the day on a lighter note. The weather helped too; sunny Mondays always lift my mood a bit. I moved between zones deliberately today rather than rushing, and it made a difference.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
